assertions.title: Concurrent first requests for one user collapse onto one VM assignment

steps.action: Create test user via passkey registration
steps.observed: User vm-concurrent-1775954922020 registered with ID f6194a4d-4afc-4ad4-9a9d-980f031a20ef
Both cookies obtained and saved to cookie jar

steps.action: Send two simultaneous protected requests to /api/shell/bootstrap
steps.expected: Both requests complete and collapse onto single VM
steps.observed: Both requests returned HTTP 200 in ~106-109ms with same sandbox_id

steps.action: Check VM ownership state on Node B
steps.expected: Single VM ownership record for test user
steps.observed: vmctl shows total_ownerships:2, active_vms:0. VM creation was attempted but boot failed.

steps.action: Verify VM lifecycle logs for concurrent handling
steps.expected: Evidence of request collapsing or single VM assignment
steps.observed: For test user f6194a4d-4afc-4ad4-9a9d-980f031a20ef: Only ONE VM creation attempt (vm-058eddc86df144f79d29dae13f9c004a) at 00:48:54. Proxy shows single vmctl resolve attempt with fallback.

sshTranscripts: Proxy logs: 'vmctl resolve failed for user f6194a4d-4afc-4ad4-9a9d-980f031a20ef, using static fallback'

assertions.issues: VM boot fails with 'no such file or directory' when creating data.img, preventing full assertion validation. System falls back to host-process sandbox mode. Only one VM creation attempt observed for test user, suggesting concurrent collapsing MAY work, but VM health prevents confirmation.

frictions.description: agent-browser does not support WebAuthn virtual authenticators, requiring fallback to Playwright for passkey registration
frictions.resolution: Used Playwright test helper with virtual authenticator in headless mode

frictions.description: VM boot failure with 'truncate data image ... no such file or directory' error persists from previous rounds

blockers.description: VM boot fails before data.img creation. Log: 'Firecracker boot failed for VM vm-058eddc86df144f79d29dae13f9c004a: create data image for VM ... truncate data image ... no such file or directory'. This prevents verifying that concurrent requests properly route to a single healthy VM.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Examined vmctl code - concurrency handling exists via pendingWaiters mechanism, but VM boot failure prevents end-to-end validation. Previous round had same issue.

blockers.description: System falls back to static host-process sandbox (sandbox-m1) when VM boot fails. This masks whether concurrent request collapsing actually works, as both requests receive the same fallback sandbox.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: N/A - fallback behavior is expected when VMs fail

summary: VAL-VM-004 testing BLOCKED due to persistent VM boot failure. Concurrent request handling mechanism exists in code (pendingWaiters with channel notification) and log evidence shows only ONE VM creation attempt for test user. However, the VM failed to boot (data.img creation error), causing proxy to fall back to static host sandbox. Both requests returned successfully via fallback, but true concurrent collapsing onto a single VM cannot be validated until VM boot succeeds. Additional finding: logs at 00:47:59 show TWO VMs created simultaneously for different user (a90fdeda-7a69-444a-adbd-bf61a827a906), suggesting race conditions may exist under certain timing conditions.

concurrentRequestHandling.codeAnalysis: OwnershipRegistry.ResolveOrAssign() uses mutex + pendingWaiters map with channel notification. First caller creates entry in pendingWaiters, subsequent callers wait on channel.
concurrentRequestHandling.observedBehavior: For test user f6194a4d-...: Only one VM creation attempt (vm-058eddc86df144f79d29dae13f9c004a). For different user a90fdeda-... at 00:47:59: TWO VMs created (vm-3161... and vm-0dd3a...) within same second.
concurrentRequestHandling.raceConditionEvidence: User a90fdeda-7a69-444a-adbd-bf61a827a906 had two VMs created at 00:47:59 with errors: 'resolve failed for user a90fdeda... failed to boot VM vm-3161...' and 'resolve failed for user a90fdeda... failed to boot VM vm-0dd3a...'
concurrentRequestHandling.conclusion: Concurrent collapsing mechanism appears to work for SOME timing windows, but race conditions exist where multiple VMs can be created for the same user before pendingWaiters registration takes effect.

vmBootFailure.error: truncate data image /var/lib/go-choir/vm-state/vm-*/data.img: no such file or directory
vmBootFailure.rootCause: VM state directory not created before data.img truncation attempt
vmBootFailure.impact: All VMs fail to boot, system falls back to host-process mode

fallbackBehavior.proxyAction: Log warning and return static sandbox URL
fallbackBehavior.result: Both concurrent requests succeed via same fallback sandbox (sandbox-m1)
fallbackBehavior.verification: request1.log and request2.log both show sandbox_id:sandbox-m1

recommendations: Fix VM state directory creation order - ensure mkdir happens before data.img truncation
Add more detailed logging for concurrent request detection in pendingWaiters
Consider race condition window between mutex unlock and pendingWaiters registration
Add test specifically for concurrent first requests with artificial delays to expose race conditions
Re-run VAL-VM-004 after VM boot fix is deployed
